sections.title: The pipeline: profile → place
sections.summary: cuVSLAM (or any adapted GPU workload) is reduced to a per-kernel placement decision through six steps. This is GPU-DAMOV: DAMOV's CPU data-movement-bottleneck method [Oliveira '21], re-derived for GPUs (two-level cache, occupancy-hidden latency, coalescing) and extended from 'is this memory-bound' to 'WHICH substrate — GPU, CPU, PiM, ISP — and if it stays on the GPU, what's the fault to fix'.

blocks.text: Every number the harness reports is defined below by the exact ncu counter it comes from and the exact threshold it is compared against — all imported live from the analysis code, so this page is the code.

sections.title: 1 · Capture
sections.summary: Three profilers, one bounded run, locked clocks.

blocks.text: Nsight Systems captures the whole-run TIMELINE (kernel time shares, H2D/D2H transfers, and cuVSLAM's own NVTX stage ranges). Nsight Compute replays a bounded window of launches with a CURATED metric set (not `--set full`, which is killed on a small GPU before it writes a report) — the counters listed in §3. NVBit binary-instruments a bounded launch window for exact per-warp addresses (§5). Clocks are locked (graphics 1620 MHz / memory 7001 MHz, persistence on); ceilings are MEASURED not quoted (205 GB/s DRAM, 5445 GFLOP/s FP32); 5-repeat CoV is 0.14%.

blocks.title: the three curated ncu metric sets (profile.py METRIC_SETS)
head: set
purpose
rows: 3 counters — duration + memory/compute SoL; a 'did it run' smoke
~15 counters — SoL, roofline, L1/L2 hit, key stalls, occupancy
~30 counters — + FLOP + byte traffic + sectors/request + full stall taxonomy

sections.title: 2 · Screen — the decision features
sections.summary: Each raw ncu counter is reduced to one interpretable feature. Extensive quantities (time, bytes, instructions) are SUMMED across a kernel's launches, then ratios are formed once — never average ratios.

blocks.name: LFMR_gpu  (L2-first-miss ratio)
blocks.note: ≈1 ⇒ the L2 is not helping (NDP-favorable); ≤0.35 ⇒ the L2 absorbs the reuse (keep on GPU). It is the GPU analog of DAMOV's LFMR (last-level-miss/first-level-miss), one hierarchy level down.  · st_track_with_cache_kernel: LFMR = 0.087

blocks.name: MPKI_gpu  (DRAM sectors per kilo warp-instruction)
blocks.note: 32 = sector bytes; `inst` is WARP-instructions (32× fewer than thread-instructions — the common trap). Memory-intensity, DAMOV-style.  · st_track_with_cache_kernel: MPKI = 31.4

blocks.name: Speed-of-Light: memory / compute / DRAM
blocks.expr: mem_sol, comp_sol, dram_sol  =  each counter, directly (% of peak)
blocks.note: bound-ness: a resource is 'high' at ≥40.0%; DRAM is 'saturated' at ≥50.0%. The mem-vs-compute ratio (≥1.5×) decides compute- vs memory-leaning.  · st_track_with_cache_kernel: DRAM-SoL = 0.3%

blocks.name: Arithmetic intensity (roofline x-axis)
blocks.note: FP32 FLOPs the standard way (FMA = 2 ops) [Yang '20]. A second roofline AI_l2 = FLOP / L2-bytes is reported too (why AI differs per level). For non-FP32 adapter workloads the numerator op-type is the one knob to change.

blocks.name: Coalescing fingerprint
blocks.note: 1 = perfect, 4 = fully coalesced, ≥8.0 = a scattered gather (the G2 signature).  · st_track_with_cache_kernel: sectors/req = 19.1

blocks.name: Occupancy
blocks.expr: occupancy% = warps_active (% of peak sustained active)
blocks.note: the GPU's primary latency-hiding knob; below 25.0% latency can't be hidden (G4/G7 territory).  · st_track_with_cache_kernel: occupancy = 2.1%

sections.title: 3 · Classify — the GPU-DAMOV decision tree
sections.summary: The features feed an ORDERED decision tree (first match wins) into one of eight classes G0–G7. Thresholds are stated once (below, imported live) and stress-tested ±25%: a kernel whose class flips under the perturbation is flagged 'borderline' and cannot carry 'high' confidence. k-means over the pooled 27-sequence feature cloud independently prefers k=7–8 — the classes fall out of the data; the tree is only the labeling.

blocks.note: The interactive tree — hover a branch for what it tests. In the Explore tab the same tree fires for a selected kernel with its real numbers substituted (one shared renderer).

blocks.label: see the tree FIRE on st_track (Explore →)

sections.title: 4 · Annotate — NVTX stages & data-structure tags
sections.summary: Kernels are placed in pipeline STAGES and their DRAM traffic is named by DATA STRUCTURE — both measured, not guessed.

blocks.text: Stages: patch 0002 compiles cuVSLAM with USE_NVTX, enabling its own profiler domains; nsys `nvtx_kern_sum` then gives the MEASURED kernel→stage table (e.g. st_track_with_cache sits under SLAM loop-closure). stages.py carries the persistence HYPOTHESIS per stage that the measurements test:

blocks.title: stage → persistence hypothesis (stages.py)
head: stage
persistence
what it is
rows: sparse bundle adjustment: system build + reduce + update
dense linear solve (cuSOLVER getrf/trsv) for BA
unmatched kernels — inspect and extend stages.py

blocks.text: Data structures: the TaggedAllocator (patch 0002) journals every allocation as CSV — `A,<t_us>,<ptr>,<bytes>,<kind>,<pc…>` with up to 12 host backtrace PCs, plus `M` maps lines (to undo ASLR) and `F` frees. attribution.py rebases the PCs, batch-runs addr2line, walks to the innermost cuVSLAM frame = the owner, and applies TAG_RULES regexes → a data-structure tag (ba_linear_system, keyframe_descriptors, pyramid_levels, images_raw, …).

sections.title: 5 · Trace — what NVBit measures
sections.summary: NVBit binary-instruments SASS to record exact per-warp memory addresses — the ground truth behind the ncu counters.

blocks.text: mem_trace instruments LDG / STG / global atomics ONLY — it excludes LDS/STS (shared, on-chip), LDL/STL (local = register spill), and the texture path — so 'locality' is about DRAM-visible DATA, not scratch. Each record carries the grid launch id, opcode, and the 32 lane addresses. Two patches bound it: LAUNCH_BEGIN/END windows the launches and KERNEL_FILTER=<substr> restricts to one kernel (ANDed → sparse kernels traced without a full-window blow-up); an alloc-events sidecar logs driver cuMemAlloc/Free lifetimes keyed by launch id.

locality.py consumes the trace: a Fenwick-tree reuse-distance pass (O(log n)/access) → a hit-rate-vs-cache-capacity CDF across 64 KiB→48 MiB (a flat CDF = the cache is structurally useless, compulsory misses); plus footprint (unique 32 B sectors), coalescing (sectors/warp), and divergence (active lanes). `--spaces {global,shared,local,all}` (default global) is what separated st_track's real scattered gather from its register-spill stream.

attribution.py joins trace ⋈ allocations: a bisect-based LiveSet maps each address to the allocation live at that launch id → its space and data-structure tag, streaming and O(1) in memory. This is how '92.6% of st_track's DRAM traffic is register spill, the rest keyframe_descriptors' is a MEASURED statement.

sections.title: 6 · Verdict — from evidence to architecture
sections.summary: The class × persistence × features map to a SUBSTRATE, and the same evidence names the CURRENT-architecture fault when a kernel stays on the GPU. This is the deliverable the whole pipeline exists for.

blocks.title: heterogeneous placement (classify.pim_affinity)
head: class + condition
affinity
substrate
rows: scatter-capable PiM — or a data-layout fix first
a bigger/persisted L2 wins; PiM forfeits reuse

blocks.name: PiM placement model (pim_placement_model.py)
blocks.expr: t_pim = t·(1−m)/c + t·m/k        offload iff affinity∈scenario and t_pim < t
blocks.note: energy ratio = ((bytes − offloaded) + offloaded/r) / bytes. Reports DELTAS vs the GPU baseline, never absolutes — and now against a MEASURED baseline joule count (§ energy).

blocks.title: current-architecture faults (same evidence, on-GPU fixes)
head: signature in the evidence
the fault
fix before offloading
rows: DRAM traffic dominated by register spill (attribution)
cut register pressure / raise occupancy — it's scratch, not data
more blocks/ILP; re-screen — memory isn't the wall
no cache size helps → near-sensor consumption, not a bigger L2
